_doc: Structured, source-traceable observation records for the weighted meta-analysis. Each numeric value is attributed to a study and (where available) a page/table locus, so every pooled figure on the page can be traced back to where and how it was derived. tools/meta_analysis.py reads this file, applies the documented weighting scheme below, and emits enrichment/meta_analysis.json. Hand-authored from corpus/corpus_extract_summary.md and enrichment/build_enrichment.py; extend via the same disciplined extraction, never by hand-editing the generated HTML. Educational resource — not for clinical decision-making.

weighting._doc: weight = class_base x ground_truth_mult x size_factor. Higher evidence class and more direct ground truth (SEEG / postoperative seizure-freedom) earn more weight, per the resource's own evidence tiers. The scheme is intentionally transparent and tunable: change these numbers and re-run tools/meta_analysis.py to see every pooled figure update.
weighting.size_factor: 1 + log10(N)/2, capped at 2.0, when a sample size N is reported; 1.0 when N is not stated (never fabricated).

certainty_tiers._doc: Derived from the number of contributing studies and the summed weight; shown as a compact certainty marker.

Loddenkemper 2005.design: Comprehensive lateralizing-sign review (Epilepsy & Behavior), Table 1 compiling named primary series.

Kotagal 2000.design: Retrospective video-EEG series of asymmetric tonic limb posturing in secondarily-generalized seizures (Epilepsia).

Roh 1996.design: Retrospective TLE video-EEG series of ictal behaviors.

Marashly 2015.design: Systematic video-PPV analysis of ictal motor sequences (Epilepsia).

Montavont 2005.design: Intracranial-EEG localization of ictal dysprosody (Epileptic Disorders).

observations.note: Forced version contralateral to the EZ in 100%.
Contralateral versive in 61 seizures / 27 patients; ipsilateral versive never observed. Non-versive turning was non-localizing.
Forced version occurred exclusively contralateral in 89% of seizures.
Versive (forced) deviation is contralateral; non-versive head rotation tends to be ipsilateral.
Contralateral in ~100% (single exception reported by Yen).
Limb dystonia contralateral to the EZ in >90%.
Contralateral hemifield in 100%.
Asymmetric (extended) side contralateral in 89%.
Unilateral tonic posturing is a 'reliable' sign (PPV > 80%); two reliable concordant signs approach 100% accuracy.
Extended elbow contralateral to onset in 94.4% (extratemporal) and 83-88% (temporal).
Extended arm contralateral in 89%.
PPV < 80% in a systematic video analysis — less reliable in isolation than version, unilateral tonic posturing, M2e, or unilateral clonic activity.
Unilateral sensory aura contralateral in 89%.
Unilateral clonic activity contralateral in 83%.
Unilateral clonic activity is a 'reliable' motor sign (PPV > 80%) for contralateral lateralization.
Postictal paresis contralateral to the EZ in 93%.
Ictal dysphasia/aphasia lateralized to the dominant hemisphere in ~100%.
Ictal dysphasia is dominant in 100%.
Postictal dysphasia lateralized to the dominant temporal lobe in 92% (p<0.001).
Postictal dysphasia/aphasia dominant-hemisphere in 80-100% across series (midpoint 90).
Classic n=100 ATL series establishing dysphasia with dominant-temporal seizure origin.

signs._stem_note: Deliberately untied from a single curated card: this is the NON-dominant counterpart of the dominant ictal-aphasia card (id 25). Preserved, identifiable ictal speech lateralizes non-dominant; dysphasic ictal speech lateralizes dominant. Kept as a supplementary meta-sign so the two faces are not conflated.

observations.note: Preserved (identifiable) ictal speech lateralized to the non-dominant side in 83%.
Ictal speech that is preserved/identifiable is non-dominant in 83%.
Non-dominant in ~100% (one exception, Janszky).
Non-dominant (right) in 81% (14/16 right-lateralized).

signs.contested: Lateralization is genuinely contested: Loddenkemper's review reports non-dominant (right) in 75%, whereas several primary TLE series (e.g. Kellinghaus) find ictal spitting more often in the language-DOMINANT (left) hemisphere - which is why this atlas's curated card localizes it dominant. Surfaced as conflicting evidence rather than forced to one side.

observations.note: Non-dominant (right) in 75% in this review's compiled figures.
Wiping hand ipsilateral to the EZ in 92%.
Unilateral eye-blinking ipsilateral to ictal EEG onset in 85.7%.
Unilateral ictal eye-blinking ipsilateral to the EZ in 83%.
Unilateral automatisms tend to be ipsilateral to the EZ; strongest paired with contralateral dystonic posturing (the classic dyad).
Ipsilateral-automatism-plus-contralateral-posturing pattern differentiates R from L TLE.
Ictal recurrent speech with altered prosody corresponded to ictal discharge of the non-dominant precentral operculum.
